bids.reasoning: We are at the very beginning of the negotiation (t=0.0), so I will ignore the opponent's message attempting to create fake urgency. The opponent opened with a bid that gives me the absolute minimum utility on all issues. Given it is my first turn, I will counter with my optimal bid, which grants me 1.0 utility across all issues, establishing a strong anchor from which I can gradually concede on lower-weight issues like issueD as the negotiation progresses.

The opponent is repeating their opening offer and combining it with fake urgency messages, which I can safely ignore since the time fraction is only 0.02. I will hold firm on my most important issues (issueA, issueB, and issueC) and make a minor concession on my lowest-weighted issue, issueD, shifting from valueA to valueB to signal a willingness to negotiate while maintaining a highly favorable utility for myself.

To move the negotiation forward while protecting my highly weighted issues, I will concede entirely on issueD (my lowest priority) by offering valueD, which matches their demand, while maintaining maximum utility on issues A, B, and C. My expected utility remains high at 0.92.
